text.title: django-quality-logging Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for Django production logging + Sentry. Each rule has a statement, rationale, and canonical source.

rule.statement: Django projects in production MUST use structlog + django-structlog. Configure structlog with the canonical processor chain (contextvars.merge_contextvars, stdlib.filter_by_level, stdlib.add_logger_name, stdlib.add_log_level, processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso"), processors.StackInfoRenderer, processors.format_exc_info, processors.UnicodeDecoder, stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter) and add django_structlog.middlewares.RequestMiddleware to MIDDLEWARE. NEVER mix structlog.get_logger() and logging.getLogger() in the same module.
rule.rationale: RequestMiddleware auto-binds request_id, user_id, ip to every log event within the request scope. Mixing logger factories produces hybrid output where half the lines are JSON and half are plain text, breaking log-aggregator query language (Datadog, Loki).

rule.statement: All Django production applications MUST configure Sentry with DjangoIntegration(), traces_sample_rate=0.1, profiles_sample_rate=0.1, send_default_pii=False, and a before_send hook that scrubs the keys ("password","token","secret","credit_card","api_key","authorization") from `event["request"]["data"]` and `event["request"]["headers"]`. Load SENTRY_DSN from environment, never hardcode. Register a Django system check that emits Warning when SENTRY_DSN is unset in non-DEBUG settings.
rule.rationale: traces_sample_rate=1.0 in production produces 10x the base Sentry bill on high-traffic services; 0.1 is the documented sweet spot. send_default_pii=False prevents automatic capture of cookies and IP, which GDPR treats as PII. The system check catches missing DSN before deploy instead of after the first 500.

rule.statement: When using an LLM to generate Django logging code, the prompt MUST include: (1) Django + Python version, (2) API framework (DRF / django-ninja / vanilla), (3) whether the service layer pattern is used, (4) explicit quality requirements (type hints, structlog over print, specific exception handling, exc_info=True on logger.error). Generic "write Django logging code" produces stdlib logging.getLogger output with bare except and no traceback.

rationale.source: empirical — Faion-network methodology authoring logs, Q1-Q2 2026

rule.rationale: LLMs default to the most-frequent training pattern (stdlib logging). Explicit constraints redirect to structlog + JSON. Without the constraints, generated code passes neither ruff T20 nor mypy --strict and requires manual rewrite.

rule.statement: Production Django code MUST NOT contain print() or pprint() statements. Enforce ruff rule group T20 in CI. Replace every print with logger.info / logger.debug using structlog and bound context. Bare `except:` and `except Exception:` are forbidden — catch the specific exception class and call logger.error(..., exc_info=True).
rule.rationale: print() bypasses the logging configuration entirely — output goes to stdout regardless of handlers. In a production WSGI/ASGI worker that output is invisible (no handler), in a dev console it leaks PII. Bare except hides AttributeError / KeyError / NameError that should fail loudly.

rule.statement: In production settings, `django.db.backends` logger MUST be set to WARNING or higher. Never enable DEBUG-level SQL logging in production. Django Debug Toolbar middleware MUST be conditional on DEBUG and restricted to INTERNAL_IPS. django-silk profiling MUST set SILKY_INTERCEPT_PERCENT ≤ 1 in production.
rule.rationale: A single view issuing 10 ORM queries at 1000 req/s generates 10,000 DEBUG log events per second — saturates log pipelines and triggers log drop. Debug Toolbar leaking to production exposes SQL, template variables, and settings through its UI.
